name: Sour Cream Orange Cake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Butter two 9-inch cake pans with 1 1/2-inch high sides.
Using an electric mixer, beat sugar and butter in a large bowl until light. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in orange peel, lemon peel, and vanilla. Mix flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a small bowl. Mix dry ingredients into the butter mixture alternately with sour cream in two additions each.
Divide batter between prepared cake pans. Bake until a tester inserted into the centers comes out clean, about 25 minutes. Cool cakes in pans on racks for 20 minutes. Run a knife around the pan sides to loosen the cakes. Turn out the cakes on racks; cool completely.
FOR GLAZE:
Bring all ingredients to boil in a heavy medium saucepan, stirring to dissolve sugar. Boil until reduced to 2/3 cup, about 5 minutes. Cool.
FOR FROSTING:
Beat cream cheese and sugar in a large bowl until light. Beat in coconut. Using a serrated knife, cut each cake horizontally in half. Place one cake layer on a serving plate, cut side up. Brush with 1/4 of the glaze. Spread 1/2 cup frosting over the cake. Top with the second cake layer, cut side up. Brush with another 1/4 of the glaze. Spread another 1/2 cup frosting over the cake. Top with the third cake layer, cut side up. Brush with remaining glaze. Spread remaining frosting over the top and sides of the cake. Press coconut onto the sides of the cake. Can be made one day ahead. Cover with a cake dome and chill. Let cake stand 2 hours at room temperature before serving.
